name: Daegu (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: the lush mountains of Palgongsan that surround the city
abundance, harvest, and Daegu's traditional textile economy

description: A green field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem composed of yellow apple-blossom petals around a circular core, with the city name beneath.
history: Daegu, a Metropolitan City (Gwangyeoksi) since 1995, has used its current flag with the apple-blossom emblem on a green field since 22 May 1995. The apple, especially the Daegu apple, has been the city's most famous agricultural product and its symbol since the early 20th century.

features.symbolism: the surrounding mountains and forests of the Daegu basin
a stylised apple blossom — Daegu's most famous agricultural product — symbolising prosperity and the city's harmony of nature and community
